title: The Tithe. To The Bride.
content:
If nine times you your bridegroom kiss,
    The tenth you know the parson's is.
    Pay then your tithe, and doing thus,
    Prove in your bride-bed numerous.
    If children you have ten, Sir John
    Won't for his tenth part ask you one.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "The Tithe. To The Bride.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
